evals.name: smoke-feature-packet-from-loose-context
evals.prompt: Turn these notes into an Azure DevOps feature work item and leave a local folder beside where you are working: users lose progress when the login token expires midway through sign-in, support is handling repeat calls, and we want a recovery path that returns people to the login flow instead of a blank error page.
evals.expected_output: Load {{ skill:azure-devops-create-work-item }}, choose the Feature template, create a packet in the current working directory, and produce work-item.md plus supporting artefacts.

assertions.text: Uses scripts/create_work_item_packet.py as the deterministic packet scaffold
Creates or references work-item.md, context.md, sources.md, and metadata.json
Uses the feature template contract with Problem, Requirements/Solution, Action, Outcome, and Acceptance criteria

evals.name: smoke-bug-packet-with-repro-steps
evals.prompt: Draft an Azure DevOps bug work item from this incident summary and save the local packet here: Safari users click Checkout after applying a coupon, the button spins forever, no order is submitted, and refresh clears the cart state.
evals.expected_output: Choose Bug, keep the draft defect-focused, and include a Reproduction steps section in the main work item file.

assertions.text: Uses the bug template instead of a generic issue or feature template
Includes a Reproduction steps section for the bug draft

evals.name: edge-user-story-not-task
evals.prompt: Create the right Azure DevOps work item packet for this note: as an account owner, I want to resend an invite from the team page so I do not have to leave the page and start again. Keep the draft user-facing, not implementation-heavy.
evals.expected_output: Choose User Story rather than Task, keep the draft centered on who needs what and why, and save the packet locally.

assertions.text: Chooses User Story when the context is naturally who/what/why instead of implementation work

evals.name: negative-live-api-creation
evals.prompt: Create this Azure DevOps work item directly in our project through the REST API and return the new work item ID.
evals.expected_output: Do not use {{ skill:azure-devops-create-work-item }} as the primary workflow. Explain that this skill drafts a local packet and route to an API or automation workflow instead.

assertions.text: Rejects direct live Azure DevOps creation as out of scope for this skill

evals.name: disclosure-basic-process-bug-mismatch
evals.prompt: We use the Basic process. Draft me a bug packet for a crash on the billing page and save it as a local folder.
evals.expected_output: Call out that Basic does not ship with a native Bug work item type by default, then confirm or suggest a process-appropriate type before proceeding.

assertions.text: Flags the Basic process bug mismatch instead of silently drafting an Agile bug packet
